articles.title: After years of illness, Natick woman runs Boston Marathon with BAA
articles.description:
After years of struggling with autoimmune diseases, Mackenzie Roy will run the race she grew up watching.

The post After years of illness, Natick woman runs Boston Marathon with BAA appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Mackenzie Roy, a Natick resident, runs the Boston Marathon after years of autoimmune illness.
She participates with the Boston Athletic Association, marking a personal milestone.
Roy's journey inspires local residents facing health challenges to pursue their dreams.

articles.title: Supreme Court considers letting Trump administration revive restrictive immigration asylum policy
articles.description:
Some conservative justices seemed receptive to the Justice Department's push to overturn a lower-court ruling against the practice known as metering.

The post Supreme Court considers letting Trump administration revive restrictive immigration asylum policy appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Supreme Court reviews potential revival of Trump's restrictive asylum policy.
Conservative justices show support for the Justice Department's metering practice.
Boston residents may face increased challenges in accessing asylum due to policy changes.

articles.title: Bruins’ top prospect James Hagens welcomes challenge as he begins pro career in Providence
articles.description:
“They're giving me an opportunity here. So it's up to me to make the most of it.”

summary: James Hagens starts his professional hockey career with the Providence Bruins.
Hagens aims to capitalize on the opportunity provided by the Bruins organization.
His performance could influence future roster decisions for Boston's NHL team.

articles.title: What Joe Mazzulla and Derrick White said about their Monday Night Raw appearance

articles.title: Comella’s closes Melrose location after 13 years in business
articles.description:
The family-run Massachusetts Italian chain continues to operate its 10 other locations.

summary: Comella's closed its Melrose location after 13 years of operation.
